title: Consider publishing a podman-from-podman feature for devcontainers.
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

It would be useful if there were a podman-from-podman (and possibly podman-in-podman) feature for devcontainers.

In the [propsed devcontainers feature spec](https://github.com/devcontainers/features), reflective of the capability in vscode, there are docker-from-docker and docker-in-docker features.  Part of this [is a spec to allow other to publish features](https://github.com/devcontainers/spec/blob/main/proposals/devcontainer-features-distribution.md)

There should be some consideration as to whether this repo or organization should publish equivalent features.

comments.body: Would love it if a community member took this on.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Sadly no one has taken this on.

Hey!

A user in our [subreddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/opire/s/d7RjREgT98) told us about this issue, saying that it could benefit from being funded in order to attract attention from someone able to solve it.

I actually use Podman as a Docker-Desktop alternative, so I'm willing to create a reward myself to incentive the resolution of this issue.

Someone from the organisation would need to install [Opire's app](https://docs.opire.dev/overview/getting-started#install-the-app) in the repository, so once that's done ping me and I'll create the reward

Hey, there just new to this community. I'm Hanshal an opensource developer, contributed to many opensource projects. I would like to work on this issue, just wanted to be clear I have extensive experience with Go. While if someone could explain me with this issue it would be great!
@Clockwork-Muse /try Id like to give it a shot as well

/assign

edit: sorry folks, i regretted trying this here. I was exploring stuff and also showing a friend without understanding the github/algora ettiquete. however, im still interested.
